source_intake_record.raw_question_text: An ideal diatomic gas has a molar internal energy equal to \( E = \frac{5}{2}RT \) which depends only on its absolute temperature \( T \). A mole of this gas is taken quasi\-statically first from state \( A \) to state \( B \), and then from state \( B \) to state \( C \) along the straight line paths shown in the diagram of pressure \( p \) versus volume \( V \). What is the work done by the gas in the process \( A \rightarrow B \rightarrow C \)?
